facts.text: Africa is the world's second-largest continent by both area and population.
Africa has 54 recognized countries and extraordinary cultural, linguistic, and geographic diversity.
The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.
The Nile River flows through northeastern Africa and is one of the longest rivers on Earth.
Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania is Africa's highest peak.
The African continent includes rainforests, savannas, mountains, deserts, deltas, and Mediterranean coastlines.
The Great Rift Valley stretches through eastern Africa and reflects major geological activity over millions of years.
Africa is home to incredible wildlife including elephants, lions, giraffes, rhinos, zebras, gorillas, and countless bird species.
The Serengeti and Maasai Mara are famous for massive seasonal wildlife migrations.
Lake Victoria is Africa's largest lake by surface area.
The continent is linguistically rich, with thousands of languages spoken across its regions.
Ancient and medieval African civilizations include Egypt, Carthage, Nubia, Mali, Songhai, Axum, and Great Zimbabwe.
Africa played a central role in global human origins, with many key fossil discoveries found there.
Modern African cities such as Lagos, Nairobi, Cape Town, Cairo, and Johannesburg are major centers of business, culture, and innovation.
The African Union became a major continental organization focused on cooperation among African nations.
It plays an important role in regional policy and diplomacy.
African Union is one of the standout topics people often explore within Africa.
Readers interested in Africa regularly come across African Union.
Learning about African Union helps explain part of the lasting appeal of Africa.
African Union remains one of the most recognizable examples connected with Africa.
